description: A query wraps an indexed column in a function or leading wildcard, so the planner ignores the index and scans the whole table.
body: Failure mode: a predicate like lower(email) = $1 or col LIKE '%term%' cannot use a plain btree on the column, so Postgres falls back to a sequential scan that degrades as the table grows. Detection signals: EXPLAIN shows Seq Scan on a large table with a selective filter; latency scales with row count; the indexed column appears inside a function. Do not add more memory or retries to mask it. Safer fallback: make the predicate sargable or build a matching expression index (e.g. on lower(email)), or use a gin trigram index for substring search, and confirm the plan switches to an index scan.
